patch.value: ContextBench: public reproducible context-quality benchmark with published methodology, baselines, and results dashboard

Turn the existing context evaluation framework into pm's strongest public proof asset. The market is full of unverified 'agents get 10x faster' claims; pm instead measures whether the agent received the right project context within budget. Extends pm-atfm (evaluation harness) + pm-lixy (judgment corpus) — never a parallel framework.

Six scenario families published: orientation after session reset, next-work selection, duplicate avoidance, budgeted handoff (1k/2k/4k tokens), evidence-backed closure, multi-worker concurrency; baselines include no-context-file, static AGENTS.md, raw task dump, pm list, pm context, pm next, pm brief, and a custom scorer (never only a strawman); deterministic metrics first (nDCG, reciprocal rank, required recall, continuity coverage, token-budget adherence, per-signal attribution — all already in the SDK); end-to-end metrics reported per model/version/client/pm-version/profile/budget/date and never collapsed into one vague claim; public /context-bench methodology + results pages and a repository; CI badge; launch claim limited to what the published corpus supports (e.g. required-context recall and budget thresholds met), no productivity percentages

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: End-to-end (model-dependent) outcome metrics to add on top of the deterministic set: correct first action, blocked-work selection rate, duplicate creation rate, evidence-linked closure rate, successful handoff rate, tokens-to-orientation, time to first correct mutation, tool-call count, recovery from context reset, conflict rate under multiple agents. Suggested public surfaces: /context-bench, /context-bench/methodology, /context-bench/results, github.com/unbraind/pm-context-bench, plus a "ContextBench: passing" CI badge. Framing line for the site: "Context should be ranked, explainable, budgeted, and tested." The existing token-budget, context-evaluation, scale, contract, external-package, and native-plugin quality scripts are marketing proof points — surface them in the same section.
